page.title: Template:YYYY crimes in countryname category header/doc

revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Intricate template}} Use this to create a category of the form "YYYY crimes in ''CountryName''", where "''CountryName''" is a valid name of a country and "YYYY" is a 4-digit year. e.g. * [[:Category:1860 crimes in the United States]] * [[:Category:1932 crimes in Japan]] * [[:Category:2011 crimes in Pakistan]] * [[:Category:2015 crimes in Nigeria]] == Usage == === Normal usage === {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header}} The normal usage requires no parameters, because the template looks up the other data. e.g. on [[:Category:1954 crimes in the United States]], no parameters are needed The other needed data is: # the adjectival form of the country in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] and adds a parent category e.g. [[:Category:1954 in American law]] if that exists # the continent(s) in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:Country2continent]] If either of those lookups fails, then an error is reported ''unless'' the required data is supplied by an optional parameter. That may be due to this category being misnamed, or it may be due to an omission in the lookup table used by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] or [[Template:Country2continent]]. (Some former countries may have been omitted. Also, when country has been renamed, the lookup uses only the current country name). If a lookup fails, then the template creates no output other than an error message. If you are satisfied that the category is correctly named, use the one of the optional parameters below. === Optional parameters === * {{para|nationality|Fooish}} bypasses the lookup of the nationality, and uses "Fooish" * {{para|continent1|Atlantis}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and uses "Atlantis". * {{para|continent2|Lemuria}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and adds a second continent: "Lemuria" * {{para|parentcountry|AnotherCountry}} is available for use when a country is part of another country.
e.g. on [[:Category:2003 crimes in Hong Kong]], use {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header|parentcountry=China}} *{{para|diagnose|yes}} turns on diagnostics, which may help if the template doesn't work as expected == Tracking == Use of the {{para|parentcountry}} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry}}) Use of the {{para|nationality }} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter}}) Categories where country lookup fails are tracked in a category shared with many other templates: * [[:Category:Errors reported by other category header templates]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Errors reported by other category header templates}}) == See also == * [[Template:YYYY crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYYY crimes in countryname category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in countryname category header]] {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Crime chronology category header templates|Country]] }}

{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Intricate template}} Use this to create a category of the form "YYYY crimes in ''CountryName''", where "''CountryName''" is a valid name of a country and "YYYY" is a 4-digit year. e.g. * [[:Category:1860 crimes in the United States]] * [[:Category:1932 crimes in Japan]] * [[:Category:2011 crimes in Pakistan]] * [[:Category:2015 crimes in Nigeria]] == Usage == === Normal usage === {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header}} The normal usage requires no parameters, because the template looks up the other data. e.g. on [[:Category:1954 crimes in the United States]], no parameters are needed The other needed data is: # the adjectival form of the country in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] and adds a parent category e.g. [[:Category:1954 in American law]] if that exists # the continent(s) in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:Country2continent]] If either of those lookups fails, then an error is reported ''unless'' the required data is supplied by an optional parameter. That may be due to this category being misnamed, or it may be due to an omission in the lookup table used by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] or [[Template:Country2continent]]. (Some former countries may have been omitted. Also, when country has been renamed, the lookup uses only the current country name). If a lookup fails, then the template creates no output other than an error message. If you are satisfied that the category is correctly named, use the one of the optional parameters below. === Optional parameters === * {{para|nationality|Fooish}} bypasses the lookup of the nationality, and uses "Fooish" * {{para|continent1|Atlantis}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and uses "Atlantis". * {{para|continent2|Lemuria}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and adds a second continent: "Lemuria" * {{para|parentcountry|AnotherCountry}} is available for use when a country is part of another country.
e.g. on [[:Category:2003 crimes in Hong Kong]], use {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header|parentcountry=China}} *{{para|diagnose|yes}} turns on diagnostics, which may help if the template doesn't work as expected == Tracking == Use of the {{para|parentcountry}} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry}}) Use of the {{para|nationality }} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter}}) Categories where country lookup fails are tracked in a category shared with many other templates: * [[:Category:Errors reported by other category header templates]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Errors reported by other category header templates}}) == See also == * [[Template:YYYY crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYYY crimes in countryname category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in countryname category header]] {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Crime chronology category header templates|Country]] }}

{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Intricate template}} Use this to create a category of the form "YYYY crimes in ''CountryName''", where "''CountryName''" is a valid name of a country and "YYYY" is a 4-digit year. e.g. * [[:Category:1860 crimes in the United States]] * [[:Category:1932 crimes in Japan]] * [[:Category:2011 crimes in Pakistan]] * [[:Category:2015 crimes in Nigeria]] == Usage == === Normal usage === {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header}} The normal usage requires no parameters, because the template looks up the other data. e.g. on [[:Category:1954 crimes in the United States]], no parameters are needed The other needed data is: # the adjectival form of the country in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] and adds a parent category e.g. [[:Category:1954 in American law]] if that exists # the continent(s) in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:Country2continent]] If either of those lookups fails, then an error is reported ''unless'' the required data is supplied by an optional parameter. That may be due to this category being misnamed, or it may be due to an omission in the lookup table used by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] or [[Template:Country2continent]]. (Some former countries may have been omitted. Also, when country has been renamed, the lookup uses only the current country name). If a lookup fails, then the template creates no output other than an error message. If you are satisfied that the category is correctly named, use the one of the optional parameters below. === Optional parameters === * {{para|nationality|Fooish}} bypasses the lookup of the nationality, and uses "Fooish" * {{para|continent1|Atlantis}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and uses "Atlantis". * {{para|continent2|Lemuria}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and adds a second continent: "Lemuria" * {{para|parentcountry|AnotherCountry}} is available for use when a country is part of another country.
e.g. on [[:Category:2003 crimes in Hong Kong]], use {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header|parentcountry=China}} *{{para|diagnose|yes}} turns on diagnostics, which may help if the template doesn't work as expected == Tracking == Use of the {{para|parentcountry}} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category: YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry}}) Use of the {{para|nationality }} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category: YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter}}) Categories where country lookup fails are tracked in a category shared with many other templates: * [[:Category:Errors reported by other category header templates]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Errors reported by other category header templates}}) == See also == * [[Template:YYYY crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYYY crimes in countryname category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in countryname category header]] {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Crime chronology category header templates|Country]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Intricate template}} Use this to create a category of the form "YYYY crimes in ''CountryName''", where "''CountryName''" is a valid name of a country and "YYYY" is a 4-digit year. e.g. * [[:Category:1860 crimes in the United States]] * [[:Category:1932 crimes in Japan]] * [[:Category:2011 crimes in Pakistan]] * [[:Category:2015 crimes in Nigeria]] == Usage == === Normal usage === {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header}} The normal usage requires no parameters, because the template looks up the other data. e.g. on [[:Category:1954 crimes in the United States]], no parameters are needed The other needed data is: # the adjectival form of the country in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] and adds a parent category e.g. [[:Category:1954 in American law]] if that exists # the continent(s) in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:Country2continent]] If either of those lookups fails, then an error is reported ''unless'' the required data is supplied by an optional parameter. That may be due to this category being misnamed, or it may be due to an omission in the lookup table used by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] or [[Template:Country2continent]]. (Some former countries may have been omitted. Also, when country has been renamed, the lookup uses only the current country name). If a lookup fails, then the template creates no output other than an error message. If you are satisfied that the category is correctly named, use the one of the optional parameters below. === Optional parameters === * {{para|nationality|Fooish}} bypasses the lookup of the nationality, and uses "Fooish" * {{para|continent1|Atlantis}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and uses "Atlantis". * {{para|continent2|Lemuria}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and adds a second continent: "Lemuria" * {{para|parentcountry|AnotherCountry}} is available for use when a country is part of another country.
e.g. on [[:Category:2003 crimes in Hong Kong]], use {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header|parentcountry=China}} *{{para|diagnose|yes}} turns on diagnostics, which may help if the template doesn't work as expected == Tracking == Use of the {{para|parentcountry}} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category: YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry}}) Use of the {{para|nationality }} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category: YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter}}) Categories where country lookup fails are tracked in a category shared with many other templates: * [[:Category:Errors reported by other category header templates]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Errors reported by other category header templates}}) == See also == * [[Template:YYYY crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYYY crimes in countryname category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in countryname category header]] {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Crime chronology category header templates|Country]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Intricate template}} Use this to create a category of the form "YYYY crimes in ''CountryName''", where "''CountryName''" is a valid name of a country and "YYYY" is a 4-digit year. e.g. * [[:Category:1860 crimes in the United States]] * [[:Category:1932 crimes in Japan]] * [[:Category:2011 crimes in Pakistan]] * [[:Category:2015 crimes in Nigeria]] == Usage == === Normal usage === {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header}} The normal usage requires no parameters, because the template looks up the other data. e.g. on [[:Category:1954 crimes in the United States]], no parameters are needed The other needed data is: # the adjectival form of the country in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] and adds a parent category e.g. [[:Category:1954 in American law]] if that exists # the continent(s) in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:Country2continent]] If either of those lookups fails, then an error is reported ''unless'' the required data is supplied by an optional parameter. That may be due to this category being misnamed, or it may be due to an omission in the lookup table used by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] or [[Template:Country2continent]]. (Some former countries may have been omitted. Also, when country has been renamed, the lookup uses only the current country name). If a lookup fails, then the template creates no output other than an error message. If you are satisfied that the category is correctly named, use the one of the optional parameters below. === Optional parameters === * {{para|nationality|Fooish}} bypasses the lookup of the nationality, and uses "Fooish" * {{para|continent1|Atlantis}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and uses "Atlantis". * {{para|continent2|Lemuria}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and adds a second continent: "Lemuria" * {{para|parentcountry|AnotherCountry}} is available for use when a country is part of another country.
e.g. on [[:Category:2003 crimes in Hong Kong]], use {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header|parentcountry=China}} *{{para|diagnose|yes}} turns on diagnostics, which may help if the template doesn't work as expected == Tracking == Use of the {{para|parentcountry}} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category: YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry}}) Use of the {{para|nationality }} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category: YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter}}) Categories where country lookup fails are tracked in a category shared with many other templates: * [[:Category:Errors reported by other category header templates]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Errors reported by other category header templates}}) == See also == * [[Template:YYYY crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYYY crimes in countryname category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in countryname category header]] {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Crime chronology category header templates|Country]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Intricate template}} Use this to create a category of the form "YYYY crimes in ''CountryName''", where "''CountryName''" is a valid name of a country and "YYYY" is a 4-digit year. e.g. * [[:Category:1860 crimes in the United States]] * [[:Category:1932 crimes in Japan]] * [[:Category:2011 crimes in Pakistan]] * [[:Category:2015 crimes in Nigeria]] == Usage == === Normal usage === {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header}} The normal usage requires no parameters, because the template looks up the other data. e.g. on [[:Category:1954 crimes in the United States]], no parameters are needed The other needed data is: # the adjectival form of the country in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] and adds a parent category e.g. [[:Category:1954 in American law]] if that exists # the continent(s) in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:Country2continent]] If either of those lookups fails, then an error is reported ''unless'' the required data is supplied by an optional parameter. That may be due to this category being misnamed, or it may be due to an omission in the lookup table used by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] or [[Template:Country2continent]]. (Some former countries may have been omitted. Also, when country has been renamed, the lookup uses only the current country name). If a lookup fails, then the template creates no output other than an error message. If you are satisfied that the category is correctly named, use the one of the optional parameters below. === Optional parameters === * {{para|nationality|Fooish}} bypasses the lookup of the nationality, and uses "Fooish" * {{para|continent1|Atlantis}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and uses "Atlantis". * {{para|continent2|Lemuria}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and adds a second continent: "Lemuria" * {{para|parentcountry|AnotherCountry}} is available for use when a country is part of another country.
e.g. on [[:Category:2003 crimes in Hong Kong]], use {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header|parentcountry=China}} *{{para|diagnose|yes}} turns on diagnostics, which may help if the template doesn't work as expected == Tracking == Use of the {{para|parentcountry}} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category: YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry}}) Use of the {{para|nationality }} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category: YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter}}) Categories where country lookup fails are tracked in a category shared with many other templates: * [[:Category:Errors reported by other category header templates]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Errors reported by other category header templates}}) == See also == * [[Template:YYYY crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYYY crimes in countryname category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in countryname category header]] {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Crime chronology category header templates|Country]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23694499169|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Intricate template}} Use this to create a category of the form "YYYY crimes in ''CountryName''", where "''CountryName''" is a valid name of a country and "YYYY" is a 4-digit year. e.g. * [[:Category:1860 crimes in the United States]] * [[:Category:1932 crimes in Japan]] * [[:Category:2011 crimes in Pakistan]] * [[:Category:2015 crimes in Nigeria]] == Usage == === Normal usage === {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header}} The normal usage requires no parameters, because the template looks up the other data. e.g. on [[:Category:1954 crimes in the United States]], no parameters are needed The other needed data is: # the adjectival form of the country in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] and adds a parent category e.g. [[:Category:1954 in American law]] if that exists # the continent(s) in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:Country2continent]] If either of those lookups fails, then an error is reported ''unless'' the required data is supplied by an optional parameter. That may be due to this category being misnamed, or it may be due to an omission in the lookup table used by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] or [[Template:Country2continent]]. (Some former countries may have been omitted. Also, when country has been renamed, the lookup uses only the current country name). If a lookup fails, then the template creates no output other than an error message. If you are satisfied that the category is correctly named, use the one of the optional parameters below. === Optional parameters === * {{para|nationality|Fooish}} bypasses the lookup of the nationality, and uses "Fooish" * {{para|continent1|Atlantis}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and uses "Atlantis". * {{para|continent2|Lemuria}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and adds a second continent: "Lemuria" * {{para|parentcountry|AnotherCountry}} is available for use when a country is part of another country.
e.g. on [[:Category:2003 crimes in Hong Kong]], use {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header|parentcountry=China}} *{{para|diagnose|yes}} turns on diagnostics, which may help if the template doesn't work as expected == Tracking == Use of the {{para|parentcountry}} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category: YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry}}) Use of the {{para|nationality }} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category: YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter}}) Categories where country lookup fails are tracked in a category shared with many other templates: * [[:Category:Errors reported by other category header templates]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Errors reported by other category header templates}}) == See also == * [[Template:YYYY crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYYY crimes in countryname category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in countryname category header]] {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Crime chronology category header templates|Country]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/25015587690|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Intricate template}} Use this to create a category of the form "YYYY crimes in ''CountryName''", where "''CountryName''" is a valid name of a country and "YYYY" is a 4-digit year. e.g. * [[:Category:1860 crimes in the United States]] * [[:Category:1932 crimes in Japan]] * [[:Category:2011 crimes in Pakistan]] * [[:Category:2015 crimes in Nigeria]] == Usage == === Normal usage === {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header}} The normal usage requires no parameters, because the template looks up the other data. e.g. on [[:Category:1954 crimes in the United States]], no parameters are needed The other needed data is: # the adjectival form of the country in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] and adds a parent category e.g. [[:Category:1954 in American law]] if that exists # the continent(s) in which the country is located. This is looked up by [[Template:Country2continent]] If either of those lookups fails, then an error is reported ''unless'' the required data is supplied by an optional parameter. That may be due to this category being misnamed, or it may be due to an omission in the lookup table used by [[Template:GetAdjectiveFromCountryName]] or [[Template:Country2continent]]. (Some former countries may have been omitted. Also, when country has been renamed, the lookup uses only the current country name). If a lookup fails, then the template creates no output other than an error message. If you are satisfied that the category is correctly named, use the one of the optional parameters below. === Optional parameters === * {{para|nationality|Fooish}} bypasses the lookup of the nationality, and uses "Fooish" * {{para|continent1|Atlantis}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and uses "Atlantis". * {{para|continent2|Lemuria}} bypasses the lookup of the continent, and adds a second continent: "Lemuria" * {{para|parentcountry|AnotherCountry}} is available for use when a country is part of another country.
e.g. on [[:Category:2003 crimes in Hong Kong]], use {{YYYY crimes in countryname category header|parentcountry=China}} *{{para|diagnose|yes}} turns on diagnostics, which may help if the template doesn't work as expected == Tracking == Use of the {{para|parentcountry}} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category: YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with parentcountry}}) Use of the {{para|nationality }} parameter is tracked in: * [[:Category: YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Template YYYY crimes in countryname category header with nationality parameter}}) Categories where country lookup fails are tracked in a category shared with many other templates: * [[:Category:Errors reported by other category header templates]] (population {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Errors reported by other category header templates}}) == See also == * [[Template:YYYY crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in continent category header]] * [[Template:YYYY crimes in countryname category header]] * [[Template:YYY0s crimes in countryname category header]] {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Crime chronology category header templates|Country]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
